findings.description: The HTML changes a key proper noun from singular to plural, altering the document's subject name.
findings.evidence: PDF subject line and body use “Roger Energy Complex” / “the Roger Energy Complex.” HTML includes “Duke Energy Carolinas LLC’s Roger Energy Complex...” but later changes multiple references to “Rogers facility dams” and “Rogers Energy Complex,” which does not match the PDF.

findings.description: One section heading punctuation is altered from a colon to a hyphen.
findings.evidence: PDF bullet labels in Section III read “Environmental Justice:” and “Dam Safety:”. HTML renders these as “Environmental Justice-” and “Dam Safety-”.

findings.description: A sentence is slightly altered in a way that changes wording and could affect meaning.
findings.evidence: PDF: “Comments also addressed suggestion of following South Carolina’s lead in how to perform ash clean-up.” HTML: “Comments also addressed suggestion of following South Carolina’s lead in how to perform the ash clean-up...” The added “the” is minor, but combined with other wording drift in this paragraph indicates imperfect text fidelity.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is generally correct, but major document sections are all mapped to h2 even though they are roman-numbered top-level sections under the memorandum title; acceptable but somewhat flattened.
findings.evidence: HTML uses one h1 for “MEMORANDUM” and h2 for “I. History/Background,” “II. March 14, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary,” “III. Written Public Comment Summary,” and “IV. Attachments.”

findings.description: One image has descriptive alt text that is somewhat generic and not clearly derived from the PDF image contents.
findings.evidence: HTML first image alt text: “North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality document background.” The PDF page image includes logo/letterhead rather than a generic “document background.” Other images have empty alt text.
